title: Rh-Negative Mother - Rh Isoimmunization Prevention
chief_complaint: 28-year-old G2P1 Rh-negative mother at 28 weeks for routine antenatal visit. First baby Rh-positive, received Rhogam after delivery. Now pregnant again. Antibody screen negative.

physical_exam.abdomen: Gravid uterus at 28cm, fetal heart 145 bpm

hidden_data.blood_type: Mother: O negative, Father: O positive
hidden_data.us: Normal fetus, no signs of hydrops

correct_diagnosis: Rh-Negative Pregnancy - Routine Antepartum Rhogam Prophylaxis
key_learning_points: Rh-negative mothers receive Rhogam at 28 weeks and within 72h of delivery
Also after: miscarriage, abortion, ectopic, amniocentesis, trauma
If already sensitized (antibodies present), Rhogam is NOT effective
Monitor with MCA Dopplers if sensitized

patient_responses.first_baby: First baby was Rh-positive, I got Rhogam after

feedback.correct_message: Well managed! Routine Rhogam prophylaxis at 28 weeks.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is routine Rh prophylaxis. Rhogam at 28 weeks.
feedback.attending_note: Rh-negative: Rhogam 300mcg IM at 28 weeks and within 72h of delivery if baby Rh-positive. Also give after any sensitizing event. Prevents Rh isoimmunization in >99% of cases.
